name: Deep Lake
tagline: Serverless database and vector store for AI, deployed in your own cloud
seoTitle: Deep Lake: Serverless AI Database & Vector Store
metaDescription: Deep Lake is an open-source serverless AI database and vector store. Store embeddings and multimodal data, run vector search, version datasets, and stream to PyTorch.
overview: Deep Lake is an open-source database for AI built on a storage format that is tuned for deep-learning work. You can use it to store and search data alongside vectors when building LLM applications, and to manage large datasets while training deep-learning models.

It handles many data types in one place, including embeddings, text, images, audio, video, DICOM, PDFs, and annotations. Deep Lake is serverless and lets you keep all of your data in your own cloud, with support for S3, Google Cloud, Azure, local storage, and in-memory storage.

Beyond plain vector search, Deep Lake adds dataset versioning and lineage, data streaming for training at scale, and integrations with popular tools such as LangChain, LlamaIndex, and Weights & Biases. It is used by organizations including Intel, Bayer Radiology, Matterport, Yale, and Oxford.
features: Serverless vector store for LLM apps: store embeddings and run vector search from your own cloud, locally, or in memory
Multi-cloud support through a single API for S3, Google Cloud, Azure, Activeloop cloud, local, or in-memory storage, plus any S3-compatible store like MinIO
Stores all data types together, including text, images, audio, video, DICOM, PDFs, and annotations
Native compression with lazy, NumPy-like indexing so data loads only when it is actually needed
Built-in dataloaders for PyTorch and TensorFlow, including dataset shuffling for training
Dataset versioning and lineage plus integrations with LangChain, LlamaIndex, and Weights & Biases

gettingStarted.intro: Deep Lake is a Python package installed with pip. After installing, you can use it as a vector store for LLM apps or as a dataset manager for training, and follow the official quickstart guides for runnable code.

steps.heading: Install Deep Lake
steps.body: Install the package from PyPI with pip.

steps.heading: Register for full features
steps.body: To access all of Deep Lake's features, register in the Deep Lake App at app.activeloop.ai. This unlocks the managed Tensor Database, the dataset visualizer, and hosted storage.

steps.heading: Build a vector store for LLM apps
steps.body: Follow the Vector Store Quickstart in the Deep Lake docs to create a store, add embeddings, and run vector search. The same data can be used through the LangChain and LlamaIndex integrations as the underlying vector store.

steps.heading: Manage data for deep learning
steps.body: Use the Deep Learning Quickstart to store and stream datasets while training models. Deep Lake's PyTorch and TensorFlow dataloaders stream and shuffle data for you, so you can train at scale without copying the dataset locally.

useCases: Storing and searching embeddings as a serverless vector store while building retrieval and LLM applications
Connecting a LangChain or LlamaIndex app to a vector store that runs in your own cloud, locally, or in memory
Managing and versioning large multimodal datasets (images, video, audio, DICOM) for deep-learning projects
Streaming datasets directly into PyTorch or TensorFlow training loops without downloading everything first
